briefdescription.para: ECC status Register.

sectiondef.header: Interrupt Status and Enable Register bitmaps and masks

description.para: Bit definitions for the ECC status register and ECC interrupt enable register.

briefdescription.para: Mask for the correctable error.
Mask for the uncorrectable error.
Mask of all bits.

memberdef.name: XBram_ConfigTable
memberdef.initializer: = { { XPAR_BRAM_0_DEVICE_ID, XPAR_BRAM_0_ECC, XPAR_BRAM_0_FAULT_INJECT, XPAR_BRAM_0_CE_FAILING_REGISTERS, XPAR_BRAM_0_CE_FAILING_DATA_REGISTERS, XPAR_BRAM_0_UE_FAILING_REGISTERS, XPAR_BRAM_0_UE_FAILING_DATA_REGISTERS, XPAR_BRAM_0_ECC_STATUS_REGISTERS, XPAR_BRAM_0_CE_COUNTER_WIDTH, XPAR_BRAM_0_ECC_ONOFF_REGISTER, XPAR_BRAM_0_ECC_ONOFF_RESET_VALUE, XPAR_BRAM_0_WRITE_ACCESS, XPAR_BRAM_0_BASEADDR, XPAR_BRAM_0_HIGHADDR, XPAR_BRAM_0_CTRL_BASEADDR, XPAR_BRAM_0_CTRL_HIGHADDR, } }

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each BRAM device in the system.

detaileddescription.para: The order must match the XBram_Config definition.

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each BRAM device in the system.

detaileddescription.para: The order must match the XBram_Config definition.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the XBram instance provided by the caller based on the given configuration data.

detaileddescription.para: Nothing is done except to initialize the InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to an XBram instance. The memory the pointer references must be pre-allocated by the caller. Further calls to manipulate the driver through the XBram API must be made with this pointer.

is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific BRAM device. This function initializes an InstancePtr object for a specific device specified by the contents of Config. This function can initialize multiple instance objects with the use of multiple calls giving different Config information on each call.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, use Config->BaseAddress for this parameters, passing the physical address instead.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XBram_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xbram.h for the definition of XBram_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Run a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: Unless fault injection is implemented in hardware, this function only does a minimal test in which available registers (if any) are written and read.
With fault injection, all possible single-bit and double-bit errors are injected, and checked to the extent possible, given the implemented hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XBram instance.
is the interrupt mask to use. When testing with interrupts, this should be set to allow interrupt generation, otherwise it should be 0.

simplesect.para:
XST_SUCCESS if fault injection/detection is working properly OR if ECC is Not Enabled in the HW.
XST_FAILURE if the injected fault is not correctly detected or the Control Base Address is Zero when ECC is enabled. If the BRAM device is not present in the hardware a bus error could be generated. Other indicators of a bus error, such as registers in bridges or buses, may be necessary to determine if this function caused a bus error.

briefdescription.para: Enable interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: is the BRAM instance to operate on.
is the mask to enable. Bit positions of 1 are enabled. This mask is formed by OR'ing bits from XBRAM_IR* bits which are contained in xbram_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Disable interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows each specific interrupt to be disabled. This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: is the BRAM instance to operate on.
is the mask to disable. Bits set to 1 are disabled. This mask is formed by OR'ing bits from XBRAM_IR* bits which are contained in xbram_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Clear pending interrupts with the provided mask.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called after the software has serviced the interrupts that are pending. This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: is the BRAM instance to operate on.
is the mask to clear pending interrupts for. Bit positions of 1 are cleared. This mask is formed by OR'ing bits from XBRAM_IR* bits which are contained in xbram_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Returns the interrupt enable mask.

detaileddescription.para: This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: is the BRAM instance to operate on.

simplesect.para: A mask of bits made from XBRAM_IR* bits which are contained in xbram_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Returns the status of interrupt signals.

detaileddescription.para: Any bit in the mask set to 1 indicates that the channel associated with the bit has asserted an interrupt condition. This function will assert if the hardware device has not been built with interrupt capabilities.

InstancePtr
is the BRAM instance to operate on.
A pointer to a mask of bits made from XBRAM_IR* bits which are contained in xbram_hw.h.
The interrupt status indicates the status of the device regardless if the interrupts from the devices have been enabled or not through XBram_InterruptEnable().

briefdescription.para: Generate a pseudo random number.

parameterdescription.para: PrngResult is the previous random number in the pseudo random sequence, also known as the seed. It is modified to the calculated pseudo random number by the function.

briefdescription.para: Calculate ECC from Data.

parameterdescription.para: Data Value

memberdef.argsstring: (XBram *InstancePtr, const UINTPTR Addr, int Index1, int Index2, int Width, u32 *ActualData, u32 *ActualEcc)
memberdef.name: InjectErrors

briefdescription.para: Inject errors using the hardware fault injection functionality, and write random data and read it back using the indicated location.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XBram instance to be worked on.
Addr is the indicated memory location to use
Index1 is the bit location of the first injected error
Index2 is the bit location of the second injected error
Width is the data byte width
ActualData is filled in with expected data for checking
ActualEcc is filled in with expected ECC for checking

briefdescription.para: Write a value to a BRAM register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit write is performed.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BRAM device register.
is the register offset from the base to write to.
is the data written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XBram_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Read a value from a BRAM register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit read is performed.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BRAM device registers.
is the register offset from the base to read from.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XBram_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: The implementation of the XBram driver's basic functionality.

detaileddescription.para: The implementation of the XBram driver's static initialization functionality.
The implementation of the XBram driver's self test function.
Implements BRAM interrupt processing functions for the XBram driver.
This header file contains identifiers and driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of BRAM devices in the system.
See xbram.h for more information about the driver.
None

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a sa 05/11/10 First release 3.01a sa 13/01/12 Added CorrectableFailingDataRegs and UncorrectableFailingDataRegs in XBram_CfgInitialize API. 4.1 sk 11/10/15 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress CR# 867425. Changed the prototype of XBram_CfgInitialize API. 4.9 sd 07/07/23 Added SDT support.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a sa 11/05/10 First release

detaileddescription.para: The user should refer to the hardware device specification for more details of the device operation.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a sa 24/11/10 First release

detaileddescription.para: See xbram.h for more information about the driver.
The functions in this file require the hardware device to be built with interrupt capabilities. The functions will assert if called using hardware that does not have interrupt capabilities.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a sa 05/11/10 Initial release

detaileddescription.para: This SelfTest is only applicable if ECC is enabled. If ECC is not enabled then this function will return XST_SUCCESS. See xbram.h for more information about the driver. Temp change
None

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a sa 11/24/10 First release 3.01a sa 01/13/12 Changed Selftest API from XBram_SelfTest(XBram *InstancePtr) to XBram_SelfTest(XBram *InstancePtr, u8 IntMask) and fixed a problem with interrupt generation for CR 639274 Modified Selftest example to return XST_SUCCESS when ECC is not enabled and return XST_FAILURE when ECC is enabled and Control Base Address is zero (CR 636581) Modified Selftest to use correct CorrectableCounterBits for CR 635655 Updated to check CorrectableFailingDataRegs in the case of LMB BRAM. 3.02a sa 04/16/12 Added test of byte and halfword read-modify-write 3.03a bss 05/22/13 Added Xil_DCacheFlushRange in InjectErrors API to flush the Cache after writing to BRAM (CR #719011) 4.3 aru 04/09/19 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Addr in XBram_SelfTest() 4.5 mn 04/12/20 Resolve build warnings for ARMCC toolchain

detaileddescription.para: None

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
2.01a jvb 10/13/05 First release 2.11a mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 4.2 ms 08/07/17 Fixed compilation warnings. 4.9 sd 07/07/23 Added SDT support. 4.14 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
